rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the actual ground-truth concern (dsocek's PEFT-naming/packed_modules_mapping point, tied to a since-removed WA not visible in the truncated diff), so recall is low for both. Both converge on the same legitimate non-blocking suggestion (derive components from _dit_modules instead of a hardcoded tuple), but Y backs it with more transparent grep-based evidence (consumer sweep, other self.unet occurrences checked and ruled out, registry.py bagel/unet divergence) making its claims more verifiable and its unique registry.py drift catch more concretely useful. X adds a plausible but less-verified text-encoder no-op observation. Both are actionable with file:line specificity and appropriately non-blocking COMMENT verdicts.
